Crime drama, starring Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team must ascertain whether the murder of a Navy officer, whose body was found on a rural road, was the result of a bar fight or because he knew too much about an attack on a fellow female officer. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and McGee begin a juice detox to purge their bodies of impurities. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of photographer Martin Roe, who was due to testify at an army first lieutenant's court-martial after he captured a picture of what appeared to be a soldier killing an unarmed civilian. However, the photograph is leaked, and Roe is later found dead with a needle in his arm(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a hotel frequented by foreign diplomats after a woman's body is found wrapped in bedsheets. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Taylor investigates the murder of a student who has been beaten and shot, and the discovery of heroin traces in his apartment leads him to suspect the killing was drug-related. The case becomes more urgent when it emerges that the victim's female room-mate has disappeared. Stella embarks on a harsh line of interrogation that results in her methods being called into question(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A routine investigation at a crime scene turns sour when Danny is attacked by a criminal hiding in the victim's house. Meanwhile, Stella investigates the discovery of a nanny's body in a Central Park public lavatory. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac gets caught in the middle of a shooting at a cafe while having breakfast, and finds himself torn between whether to pursue the gunman or help a waitress injured in the attack. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs receives a call from his old friend Senator Patrick Kiley, who claims he is being framed for the murder of a naval commander, and seeks help to clear his name. However, incriminating evidence quickly piles up against him - and a second death complicates matters further. Meanwhile, Ziva lets her hair down to obtain fingerprints from a big oil lobbyist. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs revisits his home town to investigate an assault that left one marine dead and another badly injured, and enlists the help of his estranged father to identify the assailant. Suspicion initially falls on a local man whose daughter was dating the survivor of the attack - but later inquiries uncover a tangled tale of uncertain paternity and forbidden love. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Ralph Waite(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine is found murdered in his garage, and inquiries lead the agents to his former sergeant. However, Gibbs and the team soon find out that their man is under FBI witness protection as he is about to testify against a mobster, giving him a cast-iron alibi. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Joe Spano(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A serial killer begins posting footage of his crimes online - and as the agents investigate, they realise that each film contains visual clues to the identity of the next victim. In a further bizarre twist, Abby later finds what seems to be a new video on her computer - and the case takes on fresh urgency with the discovery that it is actually a live feed, suggesting the murderer is somewhere on the premises(n)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: A female helicopter pilot is accused of disobeying a direct order to end her affair with a married enlisted man, and faces the threat of a court martial. Fortunately, a congresswoman vows to ensure she is given a fair hearing. Military legal drama, starring David James Elliott(n)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: A Marine colonel is court-martialled for defying orders from superiors, not to mention the State Department. Harm and Mac are assigned to act for the prosecution - and are shocked to find themselves pitted against Chegwidden. David James Elliott and John M Jackson star(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. While the soldier escapes with his life, the attacker gets away too. It quickly emerges the prime suspect is Qasim, an Afghan translator evidence, and evidence points against him, until it is discovered he has a brother, who is loyal to the Taliban. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones(n)

programme.title: Ultimate Force
programme.desc: Red Troop teams up with US special forces to hunt for a suspected terrorist in Afghanistan's White Mountains, reuniting Henno with his old friend, American Lt Pincher. Arriving at the villain's camp, the soldiers realise they are outnumbered and decide an air strike is the best way to expose their target, but they are left in a tricky situation when he flees into Pakistan - a country none of them is authorised to operate in(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A penniless sculptor seeks the canny dealer's help to prise a considerable inheritance out of his avaricious brother. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Ian McShane(n)

Along the way he crosses swords with a fellow officer and come to the aid of a damsel in distress. Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and John Tams also star(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The group arrives in Germany with hopes of high wages and bierkellers full of frauleins, but the truth is much harder to bear. Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais's popular comic drama following the fortunes of English workers seeking their fortune overseas, starring Tim Healy, Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Neville is suspended for lying about being a joiner, but manages to redeem himself by averting a disaster on the site. He is not only reinstated by Herr Ulrich, but allowed to work as a bricklayer - which is bad news for Oz, who is sacked to make way for him. Drama, starring Tim Healy, Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail(n)

programme.title: The Evacuees
programme.desc: Jack Rosenthal's 1975 drama, directed by Alan Parker, chronicling the experiences of two Jewish boys evacuated from London to avoid the horrors of the Blitz. Maureen Lipman, Margery Mason and Ray Mort star(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest investigates the disappearance of a parishioner who believed she was being haunted by her sister, who went missing years before. Mark Williams stars in the 1950s-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, with Sorcha Cusack, Hugo Speer and Tom Chambers(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: An ex-serviceman dies suddenly in the street, and Father Brown is called to investigate Danvers Retreat, a facility where the dead man was a patient. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The annual wine show is a major event in Brokenwood and no one is looking forward to it more than DI Mike, the recent buyer of a 10-acre vineyard. However, when a guest judge and wine critic are found dead, drowned inside a fermenting vat, the show takes second place to the murder investigation. Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson star(n)

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: Mob boss Bessie Starkman hires Frankie and Trudy to work at her illegal casino as a waitress and croupier, but Frankie ends up accused of murder(n)

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Two bodies are discovered on a life raft in Ystad harbour, the victims having been shot through the head and had their tongues burnt off with acid. The heavily tattooed corpses reveal links to the Russian mob and are instrumental in proving the men originated from Latvia, so Major Karlis Liepa, an officer with the country's police department, is sent to work with Wallander. But an anonymous phone call, a shooting and a break-in at the police HQ soon convince Wallander of a leak in the investigation - and he immediately suspects Liepa. Guest starring Soren Malling (The Killing), with Kenneth Branagh as the world-weary detective(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: A single mother from Sunderland asks Kavanagh to prosecute the neighbourhood vigilante she suspects of attempting to murder her teenage tearaway son in a hit-and-run incident. At first he has misgivings about accepting a case so far from home, but finally agrees _ only to find his already strained family life suffering as a result. Tense legal drama, starring John Thaw and Lisa Harrow, with a guest appearance by Robert Glenister(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on a 1980s music station that resulted in the death of a controversial DJ, and looks into the subsequent suicide of the station's owner. Strickland seeks a permanent replacement for the absent Halford, but Pullman and Standing call his bluff(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kerry exacts revenge on David Radford by sabotaging his planned armed robbery, but Irene turns the tables by producing an incriminating tape. Andrea and Gabriel are both given ultimatums and Adam is furious when information about the Sun Hill sniper is leaked to the newspapers(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Kerry does a disappearing act, leading to speculation that David Radford may have made good on his vow to kill her - although the gangster isn't the only one with a vested interest in ensuring her silence. Smithy eventually discovers the wayward PC by her father's hospital bed and declares his love - but their chances of romance are cut cruelly short when she is struck by a sniper's bullet while returning to Sun Hill station with earth-shattering news(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. After Kerry tragically loses her fight for life, Gabriel sets about covering his tracks by disposing of forensic evidence and intimidating a witness - but a shocking revelation could shatter his cold-blooded demeanour. Stunned by Kerry's death, a grief-crazed Smithy lashes out at Adam, swears revenge on David Radford and hands in his resignation to Gina, while DI Morrell resolves to track down the real culprit(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Already grief-stricken over Kerry's death, Yvonne faces further trauma as her sister Beverley is roughed up and Shirley reappears in Sun Hill, bringing serious trouble in her wake. Andrea fears her true identity is about to be exposed when her editor is brought in for questioning, while Samantha puzzles over a cryptic message she believes was left by Abi's abductor(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty investigates the apparent suicide of Robert's uncle, found hanged at his home, and discovers the whole village where he lived is gripped by superstition. Hetty begins to suspect that the villagers of Readsby - once a notorious haven for witches - are still engaged in satanic practices, and is determined to prove that they were somehow involved(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The elderly sleuth makes a determined effort to drum up more business, and is hired to find a woman's missing son - a schizophrenic released into the community. She faces a race against time to track down the troubled youngster before he decides there's no point in going on. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge(n)

programme.title: Lorna Doone
programme.desc: Lavish adaptation of RD Blackmore's novel of lovers, outlaws and warring families in 17th-century Exmoor. Amelia Warner stars as the eponymous heroine, with Queer as Folk's Aidan Gillen as her evil aristocratic cousin, who wants her for himself at any cost. The cast also includes Richard Coyle, Martin Clunes, Barbara Flynn and Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Lorna Doone
programme.desc: Lavish adaptation of RD Blackmore's novel of lovers, outlaws and warring families in 17th-century Exmoor. Amelia Warner stars as the eponymous heroine, with Queer as Folk's Aidan Gillen as her evil aristocratic cousin, who wants her for himself at any cost. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne receives an SOS from Holloway Prison, whose usual maternity workers have been struck down by illness, and when she and Trixie step in to help, the nun puts her reputation on the line to prevent a heavily pregnant convict having her unborn baby taken away by social services. Shelagh faces heartache when a routine pregnancy check reveals something far more serious, Alec has a confession for Jenny, and Fred takes the gang to the theatre to celebrate Chummy's birthday - only for the trip to end in disaster. Lacey Turner, best known as EastEnders' Stacey Branning, guest stars(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny has an eventful few days, starting with a patient who as well as having herself to worry about, is also struggling to cope with her mother, an agoraphobic who hasn't been outside for at least 12 years. On top of that, Alec asks the midwife to accompany him to Brighton for the weekend, leaving her wondering if his intentions are strictly honourable. However, things soon take a tragic turn. Meanwhile, Shelagh joins the local choir to take her mind off her problems. Jessica Raine, Leo Staar and Laura Main star(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack examines a case previously undertaken by a deceased colleague, who was convinced that the alleged suicide of a journalist was a cover-up by the American government. Enlisting the help of a UFO-spotter, the team discovers the reporter witnessed a mysterious air crash near a US Air Force base, and the retired officers try to track down the original personnel to get some answers. Guest starring Mark Williams, Glynis Barber, Michael Brandon and Raquel Cassidy, with Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A woman is murdered and the forensic evidence takes the team to a cosmetic clinic where the victim had been receiving various treatments seemingly for free, but Nikki and Silva are frustrated by the CEO of Hamilton Ashe hospital when he blocks access to Karen's medical records. Meanwhile, when his daughter Rosie turns up at the Lyell as her mum is in hospital over a pregnancy complication, Thomas must play the doting father for a few weeks - a role that does not come naturally(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston star in the first of a two-part story. A woman who went missing from a care home in 1986 is found brutally murdered in a burnt-out car, and the team discovers she had recently abducted a teenage girl herself. As the investigation continues, both cases appear to be linked to an urban myth about a character known as the Bag Man - an eyeless villain who kidnaps children. With Eva Birthistle, Tara FitzGerald and Wil Johnson(n)

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Two bodies are discovered on a life raft in Ystad harbour, the victims having been shot through the head and had their tongues burnt off with acid. The heavily tattooed corpses reveal links to the Russian mob and are instrumental in proving the men originated from Latvia, so Major Karlis Liepa, an officer with the country's police department, is sent to work with Wallander. But an anonymous phone call, a shooting and a break-in at the police HQ soon convince Wallander of a leak in the investigation - and he immediately suspects Liepa. programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The lads reach South Africa where they help complete the building of a medical centre, leading to romance for Jeremy, who falls for one of the nurses. Meanwhile, Andy Butcher lands himself in deep trouble after a kind-hearted gesture to help a destitute local family leads to accusations of ivory-poaching(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Yvonne is horrified to learn Beverley was assaulted by her own husband and promptly alerts CID, only for the case to take an unexpected twist when the culprit insists he acted in self-defence. Jim puts his marriage on the line by succumbing to his gambling addiction yet again, despite a stern lecture from June and threats from an angry loan shark. Jack returns to Sun Hill, determined to reassert his status as top cop, but his colleagues doubt whether he has fully recovered from his heart attack(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna urges Jess to stop pining for Zubin and get on with her life - and new anaesthetist Sean might be just the tonic she needs. Connie and Will bury the hatchet, and Owen discovers Diane is still taking contraceptive pills despite claiming she wants a baby. As Tricia undergoes her first radiotherapy session, Chrissie finds a lump in her own breast and makes an urgent appointment to get it checked out. Samantha Beckinsale guest stars(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jude and Matt face an awkward time at work after their one-night stand and Kate returns to the hospital following Trevor's death. Meanwhile, a single mother who is smuggling drugs in her stomach arrives for treatment after being dragged from the river bound and gagged. Lisa Coleman, Jason Merrells and Sorcha Cusack star(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Tough former Met officer PC Roger Valentine joins the team and immediately makes a good impression by cracking down on muggings in the Sun Hill area - but his old-fashioned attitude toward homosexuality threatens to ruffle a few feathers when he's partnered with Lance. Jim continues to struggle with his gambling addiction and chilling CCTV footage of Abi makes Samantha fear for her daughter's safety(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: On Mother's Day, Etta is concerned about Lloyd's friends, while Sharon worries about the decrease in takings at the pub(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: The group draw lots to decide who will get to have Christmas Day off duty, and Josie and Bayleaf spend the night together. Starring James Hazeldine and Katharine Rogers(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer gets involved in a con at a country house involving the sale of a fake painting in exchange for a parcel of diamonds. However, Lovejoy later realises he too has been set up over the deal. Richard Griffiths guest stars, with Ian McShane(n)

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: A recently paroled convict takes his son to Jersey without telling the boy's mother or his parole officer. Detective drama, starring John Nettles(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Adrian, who has been writing his memoirs, makes a decision that will change his life. Joey has reached the end of his tether(n)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Old Mr Grace recognises a photo of Mr Humphries' mother and begins to suspect the head of menswear may be his son and heir. John Inman stars(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean is concerned for Lionel's success as the day of his book launch arrives and wonders if Alistair will be able to help out with a publicity stunt should sales start to flag. Romantic comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy and Alvin learn that Truly's ex-wife is trying to find him, and waste no time in gleefully tormenting their pal with the news of a strange woman's appearance in town. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The chief inspector comes up against Rattigan, an old adversary from his days in London, whose legal challenge to his prison sentence has been upheld on the grounds that Gently fabricated evidence against him. Donald McGhee, the detective's friend and former colleague in the Met, unexpectedly arrives in Durham to offer support, but his motives remain questionable. With Bacchus torn between loyalty and ambition, Gently knows that it's up to him to find a solution to his dilemma. Sixties-set detective drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Kevin Whately, Diana Quick, Nigel Lindsay, Morgan Watkins and Ralph Brown(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gerry is spooked by the discovery of UCOS's latest victim - a cop, whose body has lain untouched under a basement for 30 years. As accusations of police corruption and underhand dealings begin to surface, Gerry has to face up to some skeletons in his own closet and it's a race against time for the team to help before his murky past catches up with him. While babysitting his grandson, Gerry becomes convinced that only he holds the key to the investigation and goes Awol to uncover the truth. Starring Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The detective and sidekick Jardine investigate when a prominent loan shark dies after being brutally attacked with an axe. Jardine starts a relationship with an actress who is playing Lizzie Borden in the studio that owns the apparent murder weapon as the duo come to believe it may be a revenge killing - and that there could be a link with Glasgow's Garden Festival. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Mark McManus and James Macpherson(n)

programme.title: Meripwsan

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Chelsea v Burnley at Stamford Bridge. Spurs had lost their last four meetings with Jurgen Klopp's Reds in all competitions, a record that Jose Mourinho will have been keen to remedy. Sean Dyche's Clarets had picked up four points from their previous two trips to west London, although Frank Lampard's men got the better of the reverse fixture in October, winning 4-2 at Turf Moor thanks to a hat-trick from American Christian Pulisic. Plus, action from Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park, Everton v Brighton & Hove Albion at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Southampton at the King Power Stadium, Manchester United v Norwich City at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Newcastle United at Molineux, and Friday night's game between Sheffield United and West Ham United at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.desc: Adam Partridge and Jonathan Pratt assess items at the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire, while Paul Martin finds out all about the RAF's crucial role in the Berlin Airlift of 1948(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre, upcycler Sarah Moore is on the lookout for three unloved items she can save from the skip, salvage and sell on for a profit. To help transform her trio of tip-finds Sarah turns to furniture re-styler Emma Walker to help overhaul a pair of seats, while upholsterer Anthony Devine is challenged with bringing an Edwardian armchair into the 21st Century. For her own project, Sarah takes on an old typewriter(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with designs for a social space in Corby, Northamptonshire, and a beach garden in Lee-on-the-Solent, Hampshire(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help a family of fussy eaters in Gosport on the south coast lower their food bills and change their unhealthy eating habits. James and Rianna Drew have two children, Tallulah-Belle who is three and Penelope who is one. Tallulah-Belle was born 11 weeks early weighing just one pound and now suffers with chronic lung disease. The couple spent over two years in hospital with her, living on convenience freezer food and takeaways, and now she is home their diets have not changed. Can Gregg and Chris help this family get their spending and eating habits back on track?(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live in volcanic hot spots on the ocean bed, areas that reach temperatures hot enough to melt lead and which may hold the secrets behind all life on Earth. Other strange creatures from the depths of the sea include bone-eating worms, fish that walk rather than swim, and hordes of squid. Plus, footage of sharks in a feeding frenzy over the dead body of a whale and the wildlife of the Midnight Zone in the Pacific, an area that never receives any natural light, forcing creatures to rely on their own bioluminescence(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Welney Wildlife Reserve in Norfolk, where Charlotte Smith helps out with a swan census and Steve Brown is on the trail of the elusive cranes(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Having decided that everyone could do with a holiday, the Doctor takes Graham, Yasmin, Ryan to a luxury resort for a spot of rest and relaxation. However, they discover the place where they are having a break is hiding a number of deadly secrets. What are the ferocious monsters that are attacking Tranquillity Spa? Guest starring Laura Fraser and James Buckley, alongside Jodie Whittaker as the 13th incarnation of the Time Lord, and Bradley Walsh, Mandip Gill and Tosin Cole as her trusty travelling companions(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A woman is caught stealing milk from Nonnatus House, but she pleads for mercy from sisters Julienne and Monica Joan, explaining that she has been stealing milk as she is pregnant. As Sister Julienne learns more about her, she is convinced that this woman needs help becoming the mother she wants to be. Meanwhile, Dr Turner puzzles over a mysterious string of symptoms, and the blossoming romance between Miss Higgins and Sgt Woolf is thrown into jeopardy. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Taking advantage of her newfound fame, Christine starts to forge a new career, while John Profumo tries to move on from the rumours, but his career now at stake due to his formal denial in parliament. With the establishment fearing another scandal, an investigation is opened into Stephen Ward, who finds his lifestyle is suddenly under scrutiny, and the police harass Christine, Mandy and the doctor's patients for any evidence of crimes(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents the day's Premier League action, featuring Aston Villa v Manchester City at Villa Park and Bournemouth v Watford at Vitality Stadium. Reigning champions City will have been hoping to complete a double over the Villans as they looked to keep their hopes of defending the title alive, while Watford were in need of the points having struggled at the bottom of the table this term. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Liverpool v Manchester United at Prenton Park and Chelsea v Bristol City at Kingsmeadow(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, Ryder Cup-winner Nicolas Colsaerts, Great Britain sprinter Anyika Onuora and former Celtic and Wales striker John Hartson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville hear about a villa rental fraud so sophisticated that even a former police detective fell for it. Plus, experts at the pop-up shop in Manchester help the victims of fake flight websites and overpriced American visas(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a Birmingham semi-detached and Martin Roberts follows the progress of a warehouse building in Liverpool and a house in the West Midlands town of Stafford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Gentle and Phil Spenceley experience life in Auckland, New Zealand. Gentle longs to move back to the country of her birth, but husband Phil is concerned about the uncertainties of life on the other side of the world, and worries about leaving his parents in the UK. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood introduces footage of a jeweller who refused to budge even though he was looking down the barrel of a gun, and two thieves who stole a tortoise from a zoo(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at the International Antiques Fair of Wales in Builth Wells, Powys. Charlie Ross visits the National Fencing Musuem(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's DNA results arrive, Ruhma visits an expectant mother with a tricky husband and Rob assists an intervention group by talking to youngsters about the perils of crime(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest walks in on a man standing over the body of his dead wife, while Mrs McCarthy strikes it lucky at the church bingo with the help of a fortune teller her sister has befriended. Guest starring Niamh Cusack(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson catches up a couples who bought a home in Worcestershire, before revisits one of his buyers who made the move from London to Gloucestershire(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Darren and Emma are preparing for their first-ever cattle show, but first they have to beat seasoned breeders Grania and Simon at their local Launceston Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer start their hunt in Stow-on-the-Wold, Gloucestershire, buying items including a military first-aid kit and a toast rack to sell at auction in Cardiff(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Anita Rani tells the story of one man who has devoted his life to helping the homeless. Randeep Singh started his small Southall charity with just a handful of volunteers but has now transformed it into a sophisticated national task force feeding and clothing thousands of people every week(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bex's concerns for Louise leave Lisa worried and she is more determined than ever to help her daughter. But as Louise opens up, the guilt becomes too much and she makes a shocking confession. Mick forces Linda to face the consequences of her actions, Billy shares his worries about Honey with Lola and Jean enlists Habiba's help to celebrate Kat's birthday. Ben is shocked when Callum arrives at the car lot, while Martin receives good news - the hit-and-run victim is awake(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For more than a century, the nation's high streets have been key to local communities, but with one in 10 shops sitting empty, they are in crisis. In this programme, business journalist Adam Shaw investigates the government's plans to spend millions of pounds reviving run-down town centres, and finds high streets across the country looking at other ways to remain part of their community. He learns that to adapt and change on the scale that is needed, they need to have more than just money(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a body is found in a field near the village of Hartford, Nikki and the Lyell team confirm it is that of missing teenager Jason Forbes. Working to the theory that most killers strike close to home, ambitious DCI Claire Ashby is committed to proving the guilt of her main suspect and sets her sights firmly on local locksmith Malcolm Wilde. Forensic crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, Richard Lintern, David Caves and Liz Carr, with Sophia Di Martino, Robert Pugh and Phil Davis(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Having previously embarrassing himself in a bar, Liam announces to his girlfriend Jess that he's taking a month off the booze. As he tries to make it through his first sober Friday night in years, he thinks back to where and when his relationship with drinking began as he and his mates tried every trick in the book to buy alcohol(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedian, actor and presenter Jason Manford hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by Geordie favourite Chris Ramsey and the multi-talented Doc Brown(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: British actors-turned-Hollywood stars Daniel Radcliffe and Alan Cumming discuss appearing together on the West End stage in Samuel Beckett's tragicomedy Endgame. Actor and writer Sharon Horgan chats about starring in inspirational drama Military Wives, about a band of misfit women who form a choir on a military base, and Miriam Margolyes talks about the new series of Call the Midwife. Plus, Craig David performs a track from his classic album Born to Do It, as he celebrates 20 years since its release, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson play video clips featuring a mysterious web that covered an entire street, and an Australian sea turned into a foam bath(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Lauren Layfield, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall returns to Africa in his search for the world's deadliest creatures. He encounters the spotted hyena, capable of tearing prey apart with its super-strong jaws, the gelada baboon, with a pair of fangs that would make a vampire jealous, and an animal known locally as the black death - the Cape buffalo. He also comes across the snake-munching ground hornbill and the regal crowned eagle, capable of snatching unsuspecting monkeys from the treetops(n)

programme.title: Supersized Earth
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell moves on to transport as he continues his investigation into engineering innovations over recent decades. He takes a treacherous walk along what will be one of the longest suspension bridges in the world, rides in a robot car and sees how far aeroplanes have advanced by flying a replica of the Wright brothers' glider from 1902. Along the way he discovers how to transport an object the size of Buckingham Palace halfway around the world and the advances in technology that have now made it possible to build a permanent house in space(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles between homemade fighting robots, including a towering, sword-wielding warrior built by a Mensa member and his three sons, that is one of the tallest machines to ever enter the arena and runs on wheels that are the height of a washing machine. Other contestants include a mechanical lobster and a 19-year-old self-taught engineer's creation, which has teeth that spin at 9000RPM. The conflict takes a dramatic turn when one of the robots bursts into flames(n)

programme.title: The Odd Life of Timothy Green (2012)
programme.desc: A couple bury a box containing their wishes for the perfect child they are unable to have. Soon afterwards, a mysterious boy with leaves growing from his feet shows up on their doorstep, claiming to be their son. The strange youngster soon transforms the lives of not only his parents, but their whole community. Fantasy, starring Jennifer Garner and Joel Edgerton(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Watts Gallery in Surrey, where David Harper and Thomas Plant find unusual film and TV memorabilia and some stunning French cameo glass(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at the career of the Oscar-winning actor through interviews conducted with the BBC. Hopkins talks about his approach to acting, his years as a hellraiser, and the films that helped make him a star, including Remains of the Day, Shadowlands, Nixon, and The Silence of the Lambs, in which he first portrayed his most celebrated character, Dr Hannibal Lecter. Sylvia Syms narrates(n)

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small (1975)
programme.desc: A new apprentice to an eccentric vet in the Yorkshire Dales quickly discovers life in the country is not as idyllic as he thought. However, as he finds his feet, the newcomer begins to romance the daughter of a local farmer. Drama based on the series of books by James Herriot, starring Simon Ward, Anthony Hopkins, Lisa Harrow, Freddie Jones and TP McKenna(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The keen horticulturalist visits some of the most famous and interesting gardens in the US. He begins his trek in a prairie, the original American flowering wilderness, only to find much of it has disappeared. In New York, he explores vegetables being grown on the city's rooftops, visits an enthusiastic allotment community in the Bronx and learns about the history of America's most famous public green space, Central Park. In Philadelphia, Monty examines one of the country's most famous gardens and enjoys a modern version of a prairie garden(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Architect Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin travel to Switzerland in search of four extraordinary homes. They begin their journey in a tiny hamlet called Jeurs and visit a four-bedroom house, before travelling to Brissago to see a property with views across Lake Maggiore. Next, the pair head to a remarkable 'S'-shaped home outside of Zurich, before reaching the final house overlooking Lake Lucerne(n)

programme.title: Equator from the Air
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan continues his journey around the equator, witnessing the fight to preserve the Amazon rainforest from destruction. He joins an aerial police patrol hunting for illegal loggers and witnesses a tribe mapping their territory to prevent it from being destroyed. Crossing the Andes, Gordon discovers how aerial surveillance is used to monitor Ecuador's endangered condors, and discovers a nature reserve in the canopy of a pristine patch of cloud forest, where new species are being discovered(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The world's biggest beasts have always captured people's imagination. But while being big can have its advantages, it also comes with sizeable challenges. From the Komodo dragon, whose huge appetite means it must take on prey 10 times its weight, to the giraffe, whose long neck must control immense blood pressure, nature's biggest beasts must go to extraordinary lengths to thrive. This film tells just some of these survival stories. Narrated by Miles Jupp(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring seizes the chance to make a real contribution to the war effort when his platoon is ordered to guard a captured German U-boat crew until they can be transferred to a PoW camp. Classic comedy first aired in 1973, guest starring Philip Madoc, with Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn and Ian Lavender(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: In the heart of south-east Asia lies the tropical island of Borneo. Twice the size of the British Isles, it is the third largest island on earth and home to possibly the greatest diversity of life of any island. Its huge variety of habitats, from bustling coral reefs and ancient jungles to towering mountains, have given rise to more than 60,000 species of plants and animals - many found nowhere else on Earth. This documentary covers Borneo's rich abundance of wildlife, from frogs to orang-utans(n)

programme.title: Joanna & Jennifer: Absolutely Champers
programme.desc: In Absolutely Fabulous, Patsy and Edina were rarely seen without a glass of champagne in their hands. And it turns out their real-life counterparts, comedy collaborators and great friends Joanna Lumley and Jennifer Saunders, also like nothing more than getting together to share a glass of something chilled and bubbly. Now the pair head across the channel to explore the Champagne region of France and find out how the tipple is made. Along the way, they also quiz each other about their lives, revealing previously unheard stories and anecdotes(n)

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie (2016)
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. Comedy based on the BBC sitcom, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha(n)

programme.title: Sara Pascoe Live: LadsLadsLads
programme.desc: The comedian performs the finale of her critically acclaimed 2017 show at the London Palladium, charting her efforts to be happily single after the break-up of a four-year relationship and oversharing about her imaginary brother Stephen(n)

programme.title: Love Is the Devil (1998)
programme.desc: The story of George Dyer, a petty criminal who became the model and lover of leading Dublin-born artist Francis Bacon. Hopelessly at odds with Bacon's cynical, sophisticated milieu and bewildered by the painter's cruelty toward him, Dyer sought refuge in drugs and alcohol. British fact-based drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Daniel Craig, Tilda Swinton and Adrian Scarborough(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson travels to the counties of Somerset and Shropshire as he catches up with past house hunters who have opened doors to a better quality of life in the countryside. First, Jules calls in on a couple of rolling stones who are now living the country dream in Somerset. He then heads Shropshire Hills to meet a retired policeman who wanted to get as far away from the hustle and bustle of city life as possible(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke have just one day to transform a grey and windswept roof terrace in Basingstoke into a cosy and colourful outdoor room for owner Aimee(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don journeys to northern Europe, where an abundance of botanical hot spots reveals a culture obsessed with creating gardens. He discovers the floral splendour of Britain's Sissinghurst Castle and the much painted garden of impressionist Monet at Giverny, before witnessing the midnight sun in Norway as he discovers how plants behave 200 miles above the Arctic Circle(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A compilation programme championing cottage industries, with Ellie Harrison meeting a Herefordshire woman who sells hand-crafted products all revolving around fire(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary visits Scone Palace near Perth in Scotland, where she is invited to help Lady Mansfield prepare for a special dinner with traditional Scottish reeling. She also enjoys salmon fishing on the family's private stretch of the Tay, and the regal pastime of Highland deer stalking with William, the future Earl of Mansfield and the family's long-serving gamekeeper(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: In the first episode of the series, medical journalist Michael Mosley and the team investigates how much exercise you really need to do to be healthy, and Alain Gregoire cures his lifelong phobia of heights in just two hours through the use of virtual reality. Elsewhere, Giles Yeo tests whether hot or cold drinks are better to cool you down(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Ding Junhui v Joe Perry. Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the first-round match on the opening day at Alexandra Palace in London. Ding is the reigning UK champion, while Perry was a runner-up in this event in 2017. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff heads to Iceland to compete in the world's maddest motorsport, while Paddy McGuinness gets to grips with the latest Toyota Supra. Chris Harris investigates the story of the Lotus 79, arguably the most beautiful F1 car of all time(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Ed Leigh and Graham Bell present action from Adelboden in Switzerland, where home favourite Daniel Yule was chasing a victory in the men's slalom against a stellar field that included Great Britain's Dave Ryding. Plus, highlights of the women's downhill from Zauchensee, Austria(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell assess the profit-making potential of business pitches for a home sensor system for the elderly, protein shake bottles and gluten-free cakes. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: The Speedshop
programme.desc: Following custom bike builder Titch Cormack and his team as they take on mechanical challenges to convert broken and battered vehicles into beautiful, bespoke machines. In the first edition, Titch receives a visit from a customer who needs his help. Parachute regiment soldier Chris, who just a few weeks earlier had his right leg amputated, wants Titch to build him a specially adapted motorcycle, but there is a twist - he wants to take it on a challenging off-road ride, in an attempt to prove to himself that 'he can still be the man he used to be' and that his new amputation won't hold him back(n)

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Selling Sex
programme.desc: Louis meets women legally providing sexual services, either to make a living or to supplement their income, potentially earning hundreds of pounds per hour. But rather than on the streets or in illegal brothels, these women sell sex from their own homes or hotels, utilising technology to share photos, make bookings and vet potential clients - making the exchange more accessible and, according to some, safer than the illegal alternatives. As Louis meets some of those participating in this new sexual economy, he explores whether selling sex can ever be a healthy way to make money(n)

programme.title: The Finest Hours (2016)
programme.desc: An oil tanker suffers devastating damage while caught in a fierce Atlantic storm, which breaks the vessel in two. While an engineer tries to keep the crew members trapped in the sinking wreckage alive, members of the Coast Guard brave the deadly conditions to come to their rescue. Fact-based disaster movie, starring Chris Pine, Casey Affleck and Eric Bana(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Mark Selby v Ali Carter. Hazel Irvine presents action from the first-round match on the opening day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Ding Junhui v Joe Perry. Hazel Irvine presents extended highlights of the first-round match on the opening day at Alexandra Palace in London(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Oxford, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to Cardiff to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into each before deciding which one they like the most. Narrated by Marcus Garve(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is discharged from the psychiatric unit, having made progress with her recovery - so is able to help a troubled Kian as he battles demons of his own. Max encounters a face from the past and is blackmailed into performing a dangerous surgery - if she refuses, her most painful secrets will be revealed to the world(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Sofia Huddart, who believed that New Zealand would offer her the family time she desperately needed, but her husband Nik was unsure about the idea(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Four groups of dedicated farmers who have shown resilience and ingenuity, go up against each other bidding to win this year's Against the Odds accolade at the Annual British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the quarter-final, inventing a dish that uses coffee to its full potential. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - Jay Rayner, Tom Parker Bowles and Grace Dent. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in Knockout Week(n)

programme.title: This Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day.(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Neil Robertson v Stephen Maguire. Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the first-round match on the second day at Alexandra Palace in London. Robertson won this title in 2012, while Maguire goes into the match in form, having been runner-up in last month's UK Championship. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Pubs That Built Britain
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers unearth hundreds of years of fascinating history hidden in Britain's public houses, beginning in Yorkshire. They visit an establishment believed to have been drunk dry by troops the night before the Battle of Marston Moor during the English Civil War, while in York they learn about a pub that once doubled up as a hospital and has a gruesome history.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott assess items brought into Newby Hall in North Yorkshire, while Paul Martin takes in the splendours of the country house's Adams interiors(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the West Country between the wars, setting off from the Cornish seaside resort of St Ives and ending his first in the former mining village of St Day(n)

programme.title: The Twinstitute
programme.desc: Doctors Chris and Xand van Tulleken test the best ways to beat fatigue with the help of pairs of identical twins, with Tina and Hugo trying out popular sleep regimens to find out if there is a way to combat exhaustion. Elsewhere, the participants also take part in experiments where they learn real-world skills in a virtual world, and test new theory about smart phones and the effect that their use can have on IQs(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of Edgar Wright films, gardener Margery Fish, Downton Abbey and Louis Pasteur, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Electrophiles take on the Suits. Both teams have won one game and lost another, so this is their last chance to stay in the tournament. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set consisting of The Whitsun Weddings, Homeward Bound, Oh Dr Beeching!, and Brief Encounter(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the quiz for students continues, with two teams representing their universities aiming to reach the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Exposed: The Church's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the decades-long pursuit of former bishop Peter Ball by those brave individuals determined to bring him to justice for sexual abuse, and the cover-up that went to the highest levels of the Church of England. Using powerful testimony from victims, police and church officials, and dramatic reconstruction, this programme charts the story of those who fought for many years to bring a prosecution against Ball(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Edwyn Collins reflects on the year in which he had a worldwide hit with A Girl Like You, while Mike Flowers lost out on the Christmas number one spot with his lounge cover of Oasis hit Wonderwall. Alcopops became the fashionable drink despite a storm of controversy, Braveheart and The Usual Suspects pulled in cinema audiences, and Damien Hirst touched a nerve in the art world(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Mark Allen v David Gilbert. Seema Jaswal presents action from the first-round match on the second day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Icelandic Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on a 38-mile journey along Iceland's most famous hiking route. She navigates daunting mountain climbs, red-hot lava fields, freezing river crossings and deadly clouds of sulphuric gas to reach the volcanic crater at the centre of the Eyjafjallajokull glacier(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present the ultimate guide to the Cornish coast, from the River Tamar to Tintagel Castle, as they discover what makes the stretch so unique. As well as selecting some of the best Coast stories from the past 10 years, Tessa takes a ride on a variety of boats to learn the untold secrets of the Cornish shores. The presenters go line fishing with a local Looe fisherman, explore serpentine rock on The Lizard with a leading geologist, uncover a story of tragedy at sea, and find out what it is like to live in the storm-hit harbour of Porthleven(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: With the help of the Americans and the Swedes, William and his team have found more and more victims, but the detective faces a race against time to rescue the kidnapped Line from the man they have been tracking all along. In Norwegian and English(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: William faces a fight for his reputation and his career when he is accused of tampering with evidence in a case from 17 years previously. In Norwegian and English(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Andy Crane present the edition first shown on January 5, featuring performances by Erasure, Kim Wilde, A-ha, Duran Duran, Boy Meets Girl, Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine, Climie Fisher, Inner City, Neneh Cherry, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan and the Four Tops(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Sybil Ruscoe and Simon Mayo present the edition first shown on January 12, featuring performances by the Darling Buds, Will to Power, Milli Vanilli, Duran Duran, Boy Meets Girl, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock, Mike and the Mechanics, Roachford, Fine Young Cannibals, Roy Orbison, Freiheit, Cookie Crew, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, and Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine(n)

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: The singer performs at the BBC Radio Theatre, with he and his band playing tracks from his seventh album The Time Is Now as well as classics including Fill Me In and 7 Days(n)

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: DJ Trevor Nelson and singer-songwriter Corinne Bailey Rae present their ultimate soundtrack in an hour of classic and contemporary soul and R'n'B gems. Given an airing are childhood favourites such as the Jackson 5 and Gladys Knight. as well as inspirational tracks from Prince, Mary J Blige, Stevie Wonder and Marvin Gaye. As they watch their selection, the duo reveal the reasons behind their choices(n)

programme.title: Icelandic Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on a 38-mile journey along Iceland's most famous hiking route. She navigates daunting mountain climbs, red-hot lava fields, freezing river crossings and deadly clouds of sulphuric gas to reach the volcanic crater at the centre of the Eyjafjallajokull glacier(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present the ultimate guide to the Cornish coast, from the River Tamar to Tintagel Castle, as they discover what makes the stretch so unique. As well as selecting some of the best Coast stories from the past 10 years, Tessa takes a ride on a variety of boats to learn the untold secrets of the Cornish shores. The presenters go line fishing with a local Looe fisherman, explore serpentine rock on The Lizard with a leading geologist, uncover a story of tragedy at sea, and find out what it is like to live in the storm-hit harbour of Porthleven(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: University Challenge icons Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull take a road trip with a difference, searching Scotland, England, Northern Ireland and Wales for hidden gems of British scientific and technological ingenuity. They begin with a trip to Blackpool Pleasure Beach to experience Sir Hiram Maxim's 1904 ‘Captive Flying Machines', followed by a visit to Southport, home to the only lawnmower museum of its kind in the world(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Liverpool v Manchester United at Prenton Park and Chelsea v Bristol City at Kingsmeadow(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Zoo Quest in Colour
programme.desc: A one-off special showcasing colour footage from the 1950s wildlife series that launched the career of a young David Attenborough as a nature presenter. Using new-found film and behind-the-scenes stories from Attenborough and cameraman Charles Lague, this programme features clips of the best of Zoo Quest to West Africa, Zoo Quest to Guiana and Zoo Quest for a Dragon(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould take on a double challenge as they try to prove two paintings are missing works by one of Britain's best-loved artists, John Constable. They are helping west London woman Gillie Dance, whose picture of Yarmouth Jetty turns out to have a close connection to the artist's family, and American lawyer Tom Toppin, who has been struggling to prove the authenticity of his picture, Sea Beach Brighton, ever since buying it in the early 1990s. Will it be good news for either of the owners?(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team goes back to basics to show how to enjoy the night sky, wherever you are, and Pete Lawrence joins a group of novice stargazers to talk about the best ways to introduce newcomers to the night. Chris Lintott ventures onto a roof in Oxford and marvels at the moon through binoculars, and Maggie Aderin-Pocock goes back to her childhood telescope-making class to talk basic telescope essentials(n)

programme.title: How to See a Black Hole: The Universe's Greatest Mystery
programme.desc: For two years BBC cameras have followed, Doctor Sheperd Doeleman of the Smithsonian Astrophysical Observatory and the work of the Event Horizon Telescope project team - a collective of the top scientific minds from around the world. The project combines radio observatories and telescope facilities from around the world to make up a virtual telescope with a diameter spanning the entire planet. This mega-telescope's ultimate mission: to capture the first image ever of a black hole. Although the concept of black holes has been long assumed to be fact, the Event Horizon Telescope's success would definitively prove the existence of this scientific phenomena for the first time - and provide clear visual evidence(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: To mark 75 years of the Radio 4 stalwart, another chance to see this 1982 Bafta-winning documentary that celebrated the 40th anniversary of the long-running Radio 4 show created and hosted by Roy Plomley. Featuring contributions by Paul McCartney, Frankie Howerd, Trevor Brooking, JK Galbraith, Russell Harty and Arthur Askey(n)

programme.title: Being the Brontes
programme.desc: Martha Kearney is joined by author Helen Oyeyemi and journalist Lucy Mangan to mark the 200th anniversary of Charlotte Bronte's birth by exploring the life and times of the author and her siblings Emily and Anne. Travelling to the Haworth Parsonage in West Yorkshire, the trio discover how just two years before their most famous novels were published in 1847, the sisters' prospects were bleak. Charlotte was in love with a married man, their clergyman father Patrick was going blind, and their brother, Branwell, was battling alcoholism. However, by 1848, Charlotte, Emily and Anne were literary sensations, and Martha, Helen and Lucy immerse themselves in the personal lives of the sisters to find out how this happened(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus 100
programme.desc: A century after it opened its doors, this programme tells the story of the Bauhaus art school in Germany, and the men and women whose designs shaped the modern world. The programme recounts the work of Walter Gropius, architect and founder of the Bauhaus, and the teachers and students he gathered to form the influential school(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Zoo Quest in Colour
programme.desc: A one-off special showcasing colour footage from the 1950s wildlife series that launched the career of a young David Attenborough as a nature presenter. Using new-found film and behind-the-scenes stories from Attenborough and cameraman Charles Lague, this programme features clips of the best of Zoo Quest to West Africa, Zoo Quest to Guiana and Zoo Quest for a Dragon(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the craftspeople behind the manufacture, maintenance and restoration of Steinway pianos, from the German factory workers bending the frames and polishing the veneers to longstanding British restorer Jeff about to retire from the company. Steinway supplies pianos to the royal household as well as many leading performers, and the film goes behind the scenes as renowned pianist Lang Lang prepares for a concert at the Royal Albert Hall(n)

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuela's natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again. The first episode explores the early years of the investigation from 1975, when the first Ripper murder occurred. The location of the first two murders in Chapeltown, then well-known as Leeds' main red light district, led the police to decide that prostitution was the connection between the attacks. After the second murder in January 1976, the police announced they were hunting a 'prostitute killer' and the investigation became driven by this theory(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: An insight into the development of forensic science as a way of catching murderers over the past 200 years, charting the scientific breakthroughs that have changed the course of justice. Surgeon and writer Gabriel Weston begins by looking at the difficulty of identifying the body in a murder case, from charred bones to bodies completely dissolved in acid. She focuses on the use of teeth and bite marks to identify a victim or killer, traces the uses of insects to pinpoint the time of death, and meets the geneticist who pioneered the technique of DNA profiling(n)

programme.title: The Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the North European region through its art. He begins by examining the links between landscape, climate and national character, taking in everything from the era of the Vikings to Edvard Munch's The Scream(n)

programme.desc: In order to win the quiz, Gail becomes a superhero like her teammate Captain Adorable. Meanwhile, Nana Knickerbocker and the Bees play a silly game searching for keys in gloopy honey(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells decorate Doug's bedroom. Waffle tries to help but causes a green paint disaster and escapes into Mrs Hobbs' house next door(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: While out practising her flying, Baby Skymoo crash lands in Mother Clanger's garden(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines look for the perfect place to have a picnic, and invite the Wottingers to join in the fun(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Asma Khan and Theo Randall, and Kelvin Fletcher faces his food heaven or food hell(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard is challenged to cook a calorific banquet for the Dragon Pro Wrestlers in Newport. These professional athletes pack a whopping punch when it comes to their diet eating up to 4,000 calories when preparing for a bout. It remains to be seen therefore whether Matt's vegan meal can provide them with the energy needed to get them though to an important, upcoming wrestling event(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams search for antiques at the Hemswell Antiques Centre in Lincolnshire, where experts Christina Trevanion and Nick Hall help them hunt down bargains(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Devizes, Wiltshire, designing a wildlife-friendly garden that must also be ideal for everyday family life - on a budget of £1,500, with plans including upcycling old pallets(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, Ryder Cup-winner Nicolas Colsaerts, Great Britain sprinter Anyika Onuora and former Celtic and Wales striker John Hartson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a champion cyclist while Ines is away - a case that inspires a little sporting rivalry between the two detectives. When the missing man's wife receives a ransom demand, evidence merges of a complex situation involving mistaken identity and murder(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a soap opera edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Davood Ghadami, James Bye, Sharon Maughan, Tina Hobley, Steven Arnold, Julia Haworth, Julie Peasgood and Louis Emerick(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the auditions, and the audience still hold the power, as only they will decide if the mirror opens for the acts or not. The dancers need to impress at least 75% of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition, while one Dance Captain will get to pick an act and take them straight through to the live shows. Todrick Hall joins Cheryl, Matthew Morrison and Oti Mabuse as Dance Captains, while Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo oversee proceedings(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is still to coming to terms with Mason's death when Ryan and Dante, seemingly members of opposing street gangs, are admitted to the ED. Ruby struggles to connect with baby Harmony, and living in the shared house with Marty and Jade isn't helping. Lev again comes to the rescue and, offering support to Ruby, reassures her that she's doing fine(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Chelsea v Burnley at Stamford Bridge. Spurs had lost their last four meetings with Jurgen Klopp's Reds in all competitions, a record that Jose Mourinho will have been keen to remedy. Sean Dyche's Clarets had picked up four points from their previous two trips to west London, although Frank Lampard's men got the better of the reverse fixture in October, winning 4-2 at Turf Moor thanks to a hat-trick from American Christian Pulisic. Plus, action from Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park, Everton v Brighton & Hove Albion at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Southampton at the King Power Stadium, Manchester United v Norwich City at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Newcastle United at Molineux, and Friday night's game between Sheffield United and West Ham United at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman returns with former NFL stars Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to preview the four games taking place in the Divisional Play-Offs this weekend(n)

programme.title: The Water Diviner (2014)
programme.desc: An Australian farmer's life is shattered during the First World War, when he receives news that his three sons have been declared missing in action during the battle of Gallipoli. In the aftermath of the conflict, he travels to Turkey in the hope of discovering the truth about their fate. Period drama, directed by and starring Russell Crowe, with Olga Kurylenko(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)
