programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a flood-damaged house in Carlisle, a piece of land in Breadsall Hilltop in Derby, and a semi-detached house with a great view in Alsagers Bank, Newcastle-under-Lyme, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter Q, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper help two teams in Hungerford, Berkshire, while Eric Knowles visits Sandham Memorial Chapel to find out about local war artist Stanley Spencer(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In mid-Wales, Kate and sheepdog Teg have a taste of stone-age life mucking in with a farmer and an archaeologist who are building a medieval-type settlement. Next, they explore the potential death traps of historical legacies at an old lead mine, where the South and Mid Wales Cave Rescue Team practise their skills using Teg as the damsel in distress. Kate also learns about the plight of Welsh mountain ponies, whose numbers have dropped dramatically, and discovers whether her legs are built for mountain biking(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Carlisle, exploring its Roman castle and the restored St Aidan's Church, while Radzi Chinyanganya meets a local vicar who was a convicted armed robber(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Adam Partridge and Jonathan Pratt assess items at the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire, while Paul Martin finds out all about the RAF's crucial role in the Berlin Airlift of 1948(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre, upcycler Sarah Moore is on the lookout for three unloved items she can save from the skip, salvage and sell on for a profit. To help transform her trio of tip-finds Sarah turns to furniture re-styler Emma Walker to help overhaul a pair of seats, while upholsterer Anthony Devine is challenged with bringing an Edwardian armchair into the 21st Century. For her own project, Sarah takes on an old typewriter(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with designs for a social space in Corby, Northamptonshire, and a beach garden in Lee-on-the-Solent, Hampshire(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help a family of fussy eaters in Gosport on the south coast lower their food bills and change their unhealthy eating habits. James and Rianna Drew have two children, Tallulah-Belle who is three and Penelope who is one. Tallulah-Belle was born 11 weeks early weighing just one pound and now suffers with chronic lung disease. The couple spent over two years in hospital with her, living on convenience freezer food and takeaways, and now she is home their diets have not changed. Can Gregg and Chris help this family get their spending and eating habits back on track?(n)

programme.title: The 1900 Island
programme.desc: As the families enter their second week, the weather finally turns in their favour and the men head out to sea to try for a successful catch. However, the fragile sense of community starts to fray as rivalries and hunger take their toll on the adults and it becomes a challenge for them to keep together. There's some much-needed relief when a cargo boat comes calling - but it brings more than they would have expected(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Welney Wildlife Reserve in Norfolk, where Charlotte Smith helps out with a swan census and Steve Brown is on the trail of the elusive cranes(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Having decided that everyone could do with a holiday, the Doctor takes Graham, Yasmin, Ryan to a luxury resort for a spot of rest and relaxation. However, they discover the place where they are having a break is hiding a number of deadly secrets. What are the ferocious monsters that are attacking Tranquillity Spa? Guest starring Laura Fraser and James Buckley, alongside Jodie Whittaker as the 13th incarnation of the Time Lord, and Bradley Walsh, Mandip Gill and Tosin Cole as her trusty travelling companions(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A woman is caught stealing milk from Nonnatus House, but she pleads for mercy from sisters Julienne and Monica Joan, explaining that she has been stealing milk as she is pregnant. As Sister Julienne learns more about her, she is convinced that this woman needs help becoming the mother she wants to be. Meanwhile, Dr Turner puzzles over a mysterious string of symptoms, and the blossoming romance between Miss Higgins and Sgt Woolf is thrown into jeopardy. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Taking advantage of her newfound fame, Christine starts to forge a new career, while John Profumo tries to move on from the rumours, but his career now at stake due to his formal denial in parliament. With the establishment fearing another scandal, an investigation is opened into Stephen Ward, who finds his lifestyle is suddenly under scrutiny, and the police harass Christine, Mandy and the doctor's patients for any evidence of crimes(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents the day's Premier League action, featuring Aston Villa v Manchester City at Villa Park and Bournemouth v Watford at Vitality Stadium. Reigning champions City will have been hoping to complete a double over the Villans as they looked to keep their hopes of defending the title alive, while Watford were in need of the points having struggled at the bottom of the table this term. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Liverpool v Manchester United at Prenton Park and Chelsea v Bristol City at Kingsmeadow(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, Ryder Cup-winner Nicolas Colsaerts, Great Britain sprinter Anyika Onuora and former Celtic and Wales striker John Hartson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville hear about a villa rental fraud so sophisticated that even a former police detective fell for it. Plus, experts at the pop-up shop in Manchester help the victims of fake flight websites and overpriced American visas(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a Birmingham semi-detached and Martin Roberts follows the progress of a warehouse building in Liverpool and a house in the West Midlands town of Stafford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Gentle and Phil Spenceley experience life in Auckland, New Zealand. Gentle longs to move back to the country of her birth, but husband Phil is concerned about the uncertainties of life on the other side of the world, and worries about leaving his parents in the UK. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood introduces footage of a jeweller who refused to budge even though he was looking down the barrel of a gun, and two thieves who stole a tortoise from a zoo(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at the International Antiques Fair of Wales in Builth Wells, Powys. Charlie Ross visits the National Fencing Musuem(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's DNA results arrive, Ruhma visits an expectant mother with a tricky husband and Rob assists an intervention group by talking to youngsters about the perils of crime(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest walks in on a man standing over the body of his dead wife, while Mrs McCarthy strikes it lucky at the church bingo with the help of a fortune teller her sister has befriended. Guest starring Niamh Cusack(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson catches up a couples who bought a home in Worcestershire, before revisits one of his buyers who made the move from London to Gloucestershire(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Darren and Emma are preparing for their first-ever cattle show, but first they have to beat seasoned breeders Grania and Simon at their local Launceston Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer start their hunt in Stow-on-the-Wold, Gloucestershire, buying items including a military first-aid kit and a toast rack to sell at auction in Cardiff(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen sets out to discover the truth about drink, featuring clips from the TV archives of pub acrobatics, thirsty camels and Mumbles Fizz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bex's concerns for Louise leave Lisa worried and she is more determined than ever to help her daughter. But as Louise opens up, the guilt becomes too much and she makes a shocking confession. Mick forces Linda to face the consequences of her actions, Billy shares his worries about Honey with Lola and Jean enlists Habiba's help to celebrate Kat's birthday. Ben is shocked when Callum arrives at the car lot, while Martin receives good news - the hit-and-run victim is awake(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For more than a century, the nation's high streets have been key to local communities, but with one in 10 shops sitting empty, they are in crisis. In this programme, business journalist Adam Shaw investigates the government's plans to spend millions of pounds reviving run-down town centres, and finds high streets across the country looking at other ways to remain part of their community. He learns that to adapt and change on the scale that is needed, they need to have more than just money(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a body is found in a field near the village of Hartford, Nikki and the Lyell team confirm it is that of missing teenager Jason Forbes. Working to the theory that most killers strike close to home, ambitious DCI Claire Ashby is committed to proving the guilt of her main suspect and sets her sights firmly on local locksmith Malcolm Wilde. Forensic crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, Richard Lintern, David Caves and Liz Carr, with Sophia Di Martino, Robert Pugh and Phil Davis(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Having previously embarrassing himself in a bar, Liam announces to his girlfriend Jess that he's taking a month off the booze. As he tries to make it through his first sober Friday night in years, he thinks back to where and when his relationship with drinking began as he and his mates tried every trick in the book to buy alcohol(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedian, actor and presenter Jason Manford hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by Geordie favourite Chris Ramsey and the multi-talented Doc Brown(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: British actors-turned-Hollywood stars Daniel Radcliffe and Alan Cumming discuss appearing together on the West End stage in Samuel Beckett's tragicomedy Endgame. Actor and writer Sharon Horgan chats about starring in inspirational drama Military Wives, about a band of misfit women who form a choir on a military base, and Miriam Margolyes talks about the new series of Call the Midwife. Plus, Craig David performs a track from his classic album Born to Do It, as he celebrates 20 years since its release, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In this six-part series, the chef Tom Kerridge and a group of volunteers from Gloucester embark on an eight-week weight loss and fitness journey, doing regular exercise and eating lower calorie meals in an effort to get in shape and reach their health and fitness goals. In the first episode, Tom calls the group to a sports faculty at a local university to reveal what they have signed up to for the next two months(n)

A paramedic couple in Coventry face their worst nightmare when the wife hears over the radio that her husband has been attacked and knocked unconscious while attending a patient(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bex's concerns for Louise leave Lisa worried and she is more determined than ever to help her daughter. But as Louise opens up, the guilt becomes too much and she makes a shocking confession. Mick forces Linda to face the consequences of her actions, Billy shares his worries about Honey with Lola and Jean enlists Habiba's help to celebrate Kat's birthday. Ben is shocked when Callum arrives at the car lot, while Martin receives good news - the hit-and-run victim is awake(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For more than a century, the nation's high streets have been key to local communities, but with one in 10 shops sitting empty, they are in crisis. In this programme, business journalist Adam Shaw investigates the government's plans to spend millions of pounds reviving run-down town centres, and finds high streets across the country looking at other ways to remain part of their community. He learns that to adapt and change on the scale that is needed, they need to have more than just money(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a body is found in a field near the village of Hartford, Nikki and the Lyell team confirm it is that of missing teenager Jason Forbes. Working to the theory that most killers strike close to home, ambitious DCI Claire Ashby is committed to proving the guilt of her main suspect and sets her sights firmly on local locksmith Malcolm Wilde. Forensic crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, Richard Lintern, David Caves and Liz Carr, with Sophia Di Martino, Robert Pugh and Phil Davis(n)

programme.title: Group
programme.desc: At a weekly session for post-rehab addicts trying to stay clean, Rob has some big news to tell the others, but group convenor Steph doesn't want the meeting to turn into being all about him - again. Comedy by Annie Griffin and Denise Mina, starring Grant O'Rourke, Sally Reid and Lucianne McEvoy(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Having previously embarrassing himself in a bar, Liam announces to his girlfriend Jess that he's taking a month off the booze. As he tries to make it through his first sober Friday night in years, he thinks back to where and when his relationship with drinking began as he and his mates tried every trick in the book to buy alcohol(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedian, actor and presenter Jason Manford hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by Geordie favourite Chris Ramsey and the multi-talented Doc Brown(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Asma Khan and Theo Randall, and Kelvin Fletcher faces his food heaven or food hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In this six-part series, the chef Tom Kerridge and a group of volunteers from Gloucester embark on an eight-week weight loss and fitness journey, doing regular exercise and eating lower calorie meals in an effort to get in shape and reach their health and fitness goals. In the first episode, Tom calls the group to a sports faculty at a local university to reveal what they have signed up to for the next two months(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams search for antiques at the Hemswell Antiques Centre in Lincolnshire, where experts Christina Trevanion and Nick Hall help them hunt down bargains(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brother's face one of their hardest ever garden rescues to date in Stowmarket, turning a concrete space into a family friendly back garden(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a champion cyclist while Ines is away - a case that inspires a little sporting rivalry between the two detectives. When the missing man's wife receives a ransom demand, evidence merges of a complex situation involving mistaken identity and murder(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a soap opera edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Davood Ghadami, James Bye, Sharon Maughan, Tina Hobley, Steven Arnold, Julia Haworth, Julie Peasgood and Louis Emerick(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the auditions, and the audience still hold the power, as only they will decide if the mirror opens for the acts or not. The dancers need to impress at least 75% of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition, while one Dance Captain will get to pick an act and take them straight through to the live shows. Todrick Hall joins Cheryl, Matthew Morrison and Oti Mabuse as Dance Captains, while Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo oversee proceedings(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is still to coming to terms with Mason's death when Ryan and Dante, seemingly members of opposing street gangs, are admitted to the ED. Ruby struggles to connect with baby Harmony, and living in the shared house with Marty and Jade isn't helping. Lev again comes to the rescue and, offering support to Ruby, reassures her that she's doing fine(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Tottenham Hotspur v Liverpool at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Chelsea v Burnley at Stamford Bridge. Spurs had lost their last four meetings with Jurgen Klopp's Reds in all competitions, a record that Jose Mourinho will have been keen to remedy. Sean Dyche's Clarets had picked up four points from their previous two trips to west London, although Frank Lampard's men got the better of the reverse fixture in October, winning 4-2 at Turf Moor thanks to a hat-trick from American Christian Pulisic. Plus, action from Crystal Palace v Arsenal at Selhurst Park, Everton v Brighton & Hove Albion at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Southampton at the King Power Stadium, Manchester United v Norwich City at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Newcastle United at Molineux, and Friday night's game between Sheffield United and West Ham United at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman returns with former NFL stars Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to preview the four games taking place in the Divisional Play-Offs this weekend(n)

programme.title: The Water Diviner (2014)
programme.desc: An Australian farmer's life is shattered during the First World War, when he receives news that his three sons have been declared missing in action during the battle of Gallipoli. In the aftermath of the conflict, he travels to Turkey in the hope of discovering the truth about their fate. Period drama, directed by and starring Russell Crowe, with Olga Kurylenko(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with designs for a social space in Corby, Northamptonshire, and a beach garden in Lee-on-the-Solent, Hampshire(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Another six unknown celebrities battle it out in the singing contest that has taken other countries by storm. The mystery contestants are dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes designed to hide their identities, while the panel - Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Ken Jeong - tries to guess who they are. By the end of the show, the second celebrity's identity will be revealed and they will be waving goodbye to the show. Joel Dommett hosts the proceedings(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of family and friends answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: The Brits Are Coming
programme.desc: Alice Levine announces the 2020 Brit Award nominees. With performances by some of 2019's biggest acts, including Liam Payne, Mabel, Aitch, Freya Ridings and Dermot Kennedy(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking Strictly head judge Shirley Ballas, actress Bhavna Limbachia and This Morning's resident GP Dr Ranj Singh to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. The players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charities(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Five mystery celebrities battle it out in the singing contest that has taken other countries by storm. The contestants are dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes - Chameleon, Duck, Hedgehog, Queen Bee and Unicorn - designed to hide their identities, while the panel - Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Ken Jeong - tries to guess who they are. By the end of the show, the third celebrity's identity will be revealed and they will be waving goodbye to the show. Joel Dommett hosts the proceedings(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the latest round of the blind auditions in the spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone. Meghan Trainor is the newest recruit to the judging panel, joining series regulars Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am(n)

programme.title: Shopping with Keith Lemon
programme.desc: Keith goes rug shopping with antiques legend David Dickinson and visits an Asian supermarket with singer Lily Allen, while also talking about their life and career(n)

programme.title: Fast & Furious 7 (2015)
programme.desc: After defeating Owen Shaw and his crew and securing amnesty for their past crimes, Dom, Brian and the rest of the team return home to live normal lives again. However Shaw's older brother Deckard - a rogue special forces assassin vows revenge on Dom, who he holds responsible for leaving Owen in a coma. Action thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Jason Statham and Paul Walker in his last film role(n)

programme.title: Rebound
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher hosts the quickfire quiz that tests the general knowledge of six contestants, who go head to head in a bid to walk away with a big cash prize. The players must give as many correct answers as they can in the time it takes the 'Rebound Bar' to move across the studio, with the money on offer rising accordingly(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's on a trip to Translovenia and takes shelter in a mansion owned by a vampire. In reality, this vampire is just Weasel.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean climbs into his favourite tree to stop it from being chopped down.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Fuzzypeg eats the Spuds' fish supper and Dave has to catch another. Things look up when he hooks something, but it turns out to be a whale(n)

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: The four students get too confident with their new and untested skills, and sneak off without permission.(n)

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a culinary tour of the Caribbean, visiting the islands that have inspired his cooking. His first stop is Jamaica, where his father grew up. He visits the island's famous Coronation Market and a Rastafarian farm-to-table restaurant, where he learns to make ackee-stuffed ravioli. He also takes a trip to the villa where Ian Fleming created James Bond(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Vegetarian food ideas, presented by Henry Firth and Ian Theasby. First on the menu are Sunday roast, vegan cheese in a toasted sandwich, sticky toffee pudding and snack ideas using avocados. The hosts are joined by actress Sadie Frost, who tries their jalfrezi curry(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo's latest leg begins in the Italian fashion capital of Milan, where he visits an elegant cocktail bar who were the first to introduce the idea of the aperitivo in the city. Its train station is one of the busiest in Europe and it's from here he travels on to the town of Treviglio, the home of Bianchi bikes - the first mass-manufactured bicycle in the world. Gino gets a tour of the factory before test riding one of their latest electric bikes alongside a group of local cyclists. Moving on, he visits the medieval town of Bergamo, where he meets ice-cream maker Niccolo, and visits a co-operative restaurant(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the picturesque island of Crete, intending to step away from the tourist trail and head deep into its heart to explore the Dikti mountains and its striking plateau of windmills. She meets goat shepherd Giannis, who shows her how he makes cheese using traditional methods. Back on the coast at Rethymno, Julia tries the delicacy baklava, meeting filo pastry expert Giorgos, and also meets master artisan Nico, who expertly makes stringed musical instrument the Cretan lyra. Her journey ends on the stunning but poignant neighbouring island of Spinalonga - once an exiled leper colony(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the latest round of the blind auditions in the spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone. Meghan Trainor is the newest recruit to the judging panel, joining series regulars Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Five mystery celebrities battle it out in the singing contest that has taken other countries by storm. The contestants are dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes - Chameleon, Duck, Hedgehog, Queen Bee and Unicorn - designed to hide their identities, while the panel - Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Ken Jeong - tries to guess who they are. By the end of the show, the third celebrity's identity will be revealed and they will be waving goodbye to the show. Joel Dommett hosts the proceedings(n)

programme.title: Minions (2015)
programme.desc: A race of strange, babbling yellow creatures has served the cause of evil throughout the history of the world. In the 1960s, three of these eternal henchmen get a job working for a criminal genius who is hatching a plot against the British crown - but while carrying out her orders, they uncover a threat to their whole species. Animated comedy, with the voices of Sandra Bullock, Pierre Coffin, Jon Hamm and Michael Keaton(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby host the celebrity skating challenge, joined by judges Christopher Dean, Jayne Torvill, Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman. Another six famous faces make their debut on the ice in the hope of avoiding the skate-off at the end of the show against last week's lowest-scoring performer. There is also a routine by the cast of new musical The Prince of Egypt. The full line-up of celebrities are Ben Hanlin, Caprice, Joe Swash, Ian 'H' Watkins, Kevin Kilbane, Libby Clegg, Lisa George, Lucrezia Millarini, Maura Higgins, Perri Kiely, Trisha Goddard and Radzi Chinyanganya(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Vera Stanhope investigates the case of murdered entrepreneur Freddie Gill, whose body is found by bailiffs attempting to repossess his house. As she delves into the case, she soon comes to understand that Freddie's professional life is just as turbulent as his family life, and Vera must uncover whether the motive behind Freddie's death comes from the trouble at the heart of his family, a desperate financial crises, or his glances with organised crime. Starring Brenda Blethyn, Kenny Doughty and Jon Morrison(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together, beginning their journey in Florida, the southernmost point of the country. From there, the pair head to the Palm Beach International Raceway, before getting up close and personal with sharks(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: An American airline has one of its busiest days as hundreds of people fly to the USA for Thanksgiving, while Heathrow employee Sunita helps a woman who has lost her two-year old daughter. Ground staff use measures to disperse birds from the runway to prevent any collisions with aircraft, resident paramedic Izzy treats a pregnant passenger suffering from serious abdominal pain who is due to fly to Brazil that day and British Airways employees prepare for the inaugural flight of their new £185 million aircraft the Dreamliner(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Recently single teacher Laura Nielson is set up with Andrew Earlham, a widowed surgeon and the father of one of her students. The two enjoy a first date, but the next day rumours and accusations begin to circulate and the two find themselves entangled in a web of deceit and confusion. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Shelley Conn, Zoe Tapper and Warren Brown(n)

programme.title: Teach My Pet to Do That
programme.desc: Animal lover, comedian and presenter Alexander Armstrong opens the doors of his multispecies pet training school, greeting Kevin the chow chow dog and Rambo the miniature New Zealand kunekune pig, to see if they can be taught to beg, spin and roll over. This week's star pet is Pudsey, the first dog to win Britain's Got Talent and which died recently. Plus, the show's trainers hit the road to see if they can train two chickens to play the world's most unusual game of Celebrity Guess Who by attempting to pick out the faces of Ant McPartlin and Katie Price from a constellation of stars(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Tony Blackburn talks about the success of BBC Radio 2's Sounds of the 60s Live, which toured the UK throughout 2019 and a raft of new dates have been added for 2020(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and topical studio discussion from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and the team are in Milton Keynes, where a ceramic egg attracts David Ford's attention, Tracy Thackray-Howitt buys a gold cigarette case and Mark Stevens eyes up some Iranian portraits in gold(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five members of a family from Lancashire answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants James, Joe, Shelley and Helen pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ryan confronts Graham, Aaron receives troubling news and Jai is feeling the pressure(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz goes back to the police and reveals how she unwittingly bought a gun as part of a house clearance, it disappeared and she panicked. Cathy quizzes Yasmeen about her cleaning obsession, and Aggie confides in Ed that she's considering returning to nursing(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: It can get pretty tough in January with Christmas bills flooding through the nation's letterboxes, so the financial journalist and Angellica Bell are on hand to help navigate new regulations relating to overdrafts. Plus, there is a look at consumer rights when asking for a refund(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Cathy confides in Brian how she witnessed Geoff being nasty to Yasmeen, and a tearful Fiz concedes that she'll stay in a B&B so the girls can return home. In an attempt to rebuild their marriage, Steve gets down on one knee and asks Tracy to re-marry him(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam finds himself in hot water at work, while his relationship with Karen continues to affect the group. Jenny has finished her cancer treatment, but her struggle is far from over, while David begins to see Mary in a new light. The return of Mike Bullen's comedy drama, starring James Nesbitt, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and Robert Bathurst(n)

programme.title: Welcome to HMP Belmarsh with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: Part one of two. The documentary maker goes inside the walls of HMP Belmarsh, the country's most notorious maximum-security jail, which has housed the country's most dangerous - and infamous - convicts, including Ian Huntley, Ronnie Biggs and Charles Bronson. Ross explores how staff cope with high-profile inmates, extremists and common criminals living side-by-side and goes inside the High Security Unit, where he meets Muhammad Asif Hafeez, known as Sultan, the alleged mastermind of a drug-smuggling empire(n)

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: With Christmas behind them, John Torode and Lisa Faulkner present some light and fresh recipes to kick off the new year. They start with home-made granola and stewed fruit, Lisa makes a one-pot vegetarian pasta dish and a spiced cod tray bake and John demonstrates pearl barley with an array of mushrooms(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha is joined by chef Eran Tibi to dish up a family feast from the Middle East. The menu comprises an Israeli mezze of stuffed brick and sweet potato hummus for breakfast, mussakhan for lunch, Eran's aubergine, tomato and beef stew for dinner and Nadia's cigar baklava with cheat's pistachio ice cream for dessert(n)

programme.desc: An American airline has one of its busiest days as hundreds of people fly to the USA for Thanksgiving, while Heathrow employee Sunita helps a woman who has lost her two-year old daughter. Ground staff use measures to disperse birds from the runway to prevent any collisions with aircraft, resident paramedic Izzy treats a pregnant passenger suffering from serious abdominal pain who is due to fly to Brazil that day and British Airways employees prepare for the inaugural flight of their new £185 million aircraft the Dreamliner(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Recently single teacher Laura Nielson is set up with Andrew Earlham, a widowed surgeon and the father of one of her students. The two enjoy a first date, but the next day rumours and accusations begin to circulate and the two find themselves entangled in a web of deceit and confusion. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Shelley Conn, Zoe Tapper and Warren Brown(n)

programme.title: Teach My Pet to Do That
programme.desc: Animal lover, comedian and presenter Alexander Armstrong opens the doors of his multispecies pet training school, greeting Kevin the chow chow dog and Rambo the miniature New Zealand kunekune pig, to see if they can be taught to beg, spin and roll over. This week's star pet is Pudsey, the first dog to win Britain's Got Talent and which died recently. Plus, the show's trainers hit the road to see if they can train two chickens to play the world's most unusual game of Celebrity Guess Who by attempting to pick out the faces of Ant McPartlin and Katie Price from a constellation of stars(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: It can get pretty tough in January with Christmas bills flooding through the nation's letterboxes, so the financial journalist and Angellica Bell are on hand to help navigate new regulations relating to overdrafts. Plus, there is a look at consumer rights when asking for a refund(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Cathy confides in Brian how she witnessed Geoff being nasty to Yasmeen, and a tearful Fiz concedes that she'll stay in a B&B so the girls can return home. In an attempt to rebuild their marriage, Steve gets down on one knee and asks Tracy to re-marry him(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam finds himself in hot water at work, while his relationship with Karen continues to affect the group. Jenny has finished her cancer treatment, but her struggle is far from over, while David begins to see Mary in a new light. The return of Mike Bullen's comedy drama, starring James Nesbitt, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and Robert Bathurst(n)

programme.title: Welcome to HMP Belmarsh with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: Part one of two. The documentary maker goes inside the walls of HMP Belmarsh, the country's most notorious maximum-security jail, which has housed the country's most dangerous - and infamous - convicts, including Ian Huntley, Ronnie Biggs and Charles Bronson. Ross explores how staff cope with high-profile inmates, extremists and common criminals living side-by-side and goes inside the High Security Unit, where he meets Muhammad Asif Hafeez, known as Sultan, the alleged mastermind of a drug-smuggling empire(n)

programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Members of the Midsomer-in-the-Marsh Ornithological Society fall out when Ralph Ford claims to have spotted a blue-crested hoopoe, meaning he wins the group's year-listing competition. However, their president Patrick Morgan says the rare species could never have been seen so far away from its native Uganda and brands him a cheat. The next day, Barnaby and Jones have a murder case on their hands when Patrick is found dead at the scene of the reported sighting. Guest starring Paul Nicholls and James Dreyfus(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Peter Gunn, a 39-year-old man whose birth mother gave him up for adoption when he was only nine months old, sets out to track down his biological mum. For years, Peter struggled with a sense of rejection as a result of being parted from his natural parents at such an early age, but having overcome his resentments, he now seeks to answer some burning questions about his past. Meanwhile, 71-year-old north Wales resident Ron Williams asks for help in his lifelong quest to track down the estranged younger sister with whom he spent an idyllic childhood - until she was adopted away from the family. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with James Callis, William Gaunt and Raquel Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary offering a behind the scenes look at life inside the Ritz Hotel in London, one of the most famous, traditional luxury hotels in the world. The programmes follows some of the famous faces that visit the hotels, including Richard E Grant, Anna Wintour and Mo Salah, and the work of hotel staff as they prepare for their famous afternoon tea. With consumers spending less and ever more luxury hotels opening their doors, how does one of the most well-known hotels in the world stand out from the rest?(n)

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Executive chef John Williams prepares to launch the first-ever cookbook that reveals The Ritz's most famous recipes. Penny Lancaster pops in for cocktails, and head concierge Michael De Cozar is celebrated for his 46 years of service(n)

programme.title: Charles: 50 Years a Prince
programme.desc: Fifty years ago in Caernarfon at his investiture, Prince Charles embarked on a unique relationship with a country that has shaped his life and work. While acknowledging a sometimes troubled and turbulent history, this documentary celebrates Wales through the eyes of a prince who has made the role his own. Narrated by Luke Evans(n)

programme.title: Cameraman to the Queen
programme.desc: Documentary following royal cameraman Peter Wilkinson as he films the Queen at key engagements over the course of one year. The videographer provides an insight into his unique relationship with the monarch and discusses key moments he has caught on tape, including the days following the death of the Queen Mother in 2001 and the aftermath of the London bombings in July 2005(n)

programme.title: Changeling (2008)
programme.desc: A single mother in 1920s Los Angeles is distraught when her son goes missing. After months of searching, the police claim to have found him, but she insists the boy is an imposter. She resolves to prove the police are trying to bury the case, but they respond by slandering her as a hysterical and unfit mother, leading to her confinement in a mental hospital. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, with Angelina Jolie and John Malkovich(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster leaves central Manchester, heading east along the Rochdale Canal. Not long into his journey, John finds himself struggling to contend with 19th-century technology at a cotton works. Further up the canal, he helps out members of an conservation group clearing the waterway of dumped supermarket trollies. After passing into West Yorkshire, it's on to Hebden Bridge, famous for the hard-wearing material corduroy, before John tries his hand at clog-making in one of Britain's few remaining factories dedicated to the craft(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bertie's injections prove an ordeal for Daniel. Fiz and Tyrone are questioned about the Christmas Day shootings. Sally's hot tub hosts an awkward reunion for Tracy(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Members of the Midsomer-in-the-Marsh Ornithological Society fall out when Ralph Ford claims to have spotted a blue-crested hoopoe, meaning he wins the group's year-listing competition. However, their president Patrick Morgan says the rare species could never have been seen so far away from its native Uganda and brands him a cheat. The next day, Barnaby and Jones have a murder case on their hands when Patrick is found dead at the scene of the reported sighting. Guest starring Paul Nicholls and James Dreyfus(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby becomes personally involved in the case of an abandoned baby that is brought to the hospital during the Holi festival, and Lydia is reunited with an expat artist, whose unusual lifestyle may be about to catch up with him. Medical drama set in coastal town in tropical South India, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ram clashes with Lydia as a medical case becomes personal on the day he is due to attend a lavish wedding. Ruby treats a patient from the UK whose story does not add up, and the experience leaves her wondering where she really belongs(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Peter Gunn, a 39-year-old man whose birth mother gave him up for adoption when he was only nine months old, sets out to track down his biological mum. For years, Peter struggled with a sense of rejection as a result of being parted from his natural parents at such an early age, but having overcome his resentments, he now seeks to answer some burning questions about his past. Meanwhile, 71-year-old north Wales resident Ron Williams asks for help in his lifelong quest to track down the estranged younger sister with whom he spent an idyllic childhood - until she was adopted away from the family. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a dilemma when a murder investigation leads him to the radar station where his missing son was working on a highly classified operation - just as Hitler launches a major offensive on Britain's RAF airfields. To solve the mystery, Foyle must penetrate the closed ranks of the airforce. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Julian Ovenden, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Calf, Roger Allam and Anton Lesser(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The year is 1940, and Sam is made homeless when a bomb destroys her boarding house, alerting Foyle to a system of organised looting around Hastings. However, when a corpse is found on the beach, the investigation brings the team into conflict with British attempts to secure vital American aid for the war effort. Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Bentham DeQuetteville falls to his death from a roof after seeing a headless horseman, his aristocratic family seem more concerned about their forthcoming Civil War re-enactment than the incident. As Barnaby and Jones try to discover the truth behind the ghostly figure, their investigation uncovers shocking secrets about the DeQuettevilles. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with James Callis, William Gaunt and Raquel Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary offering a behind the scenes look at life inside the Ritz Hotel in London, one of the most famous, traditional luxury hotels in the world. The programmes follows some of the famous faces that visit the hotels, including Richard E Grant, Anna Wintour and Mo Salah, and the work of hotel staff as they prepare for their famous afternoon tea. With consumers spending less and ever more luxury hotels opening their doors, how does one of the most well-known hotels in the world stand out from the rest?(n)

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Executive chef John Williams prepares to launch the first-ever cookbook that reveals The Ritz's most famous recipes. Penny Lancaster pops in for cocktails, and head concierge Michael De Cozar is celebrated for his 46 years of service(n)

programme.title: Charles: 50 Years a Prince
programme.desc: Fifty years ago in Caernarfon at his investiture, Prince Charles embarked on a unique relationship with a country that has shaped his life and work. While acknowledging a sometimes troubled and turbulent history, this documentary celebrates Wales through the eyes of a prince who has made the role his own. Narrated by Luke Evans(n)

programme.title: Cameraman to the Queen
programme.desc: Documentary following royal cameraman Peter Wilkinson as he films the Queen at key engagements over the course of one year. The videographer provides an insight into his unique relationship with the monarch and discusses key moments he has caught on tape, including the days following the death of the Queen Mother in 2001 and the aftermath of the London bombings in July 2005(n)

programme.title: Changeling (2008)
programme.desc: A single mother in 1920s Los Angeles is distraught when her son goes missing. After months of searching, the police claim to have found him, but she insists the boy is an imposter. She resolves to prove the police are trying to bury the case, but they respond by slandering her as a hysterical and unfit mother, leading to her confinement in a mental hospital. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, with Angelina Jolie and John Malkovich(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster leaves central Manchester, heading east along the Rochdale Canal. Not long into his journey, John finds himself struggling to contend with 19th-century technology at a cotton works. Further up the canal, he helps out members of an conservation group clearing the waterway of dumped supermarket trollies. After passing into West Yorkshire, it's on to Hebden Bridge, famous for the hard-wearing material corduroy, before John tries his hand at clog-making in one of Britain's few remaining factories dedicated to the craft(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: In 1935, beleaguered Bournemouth mother Louisa Durrell decides to uproot her family and build a new life on the sun-drenched island of Corfu. However, with money problems and four unruly children to care for, even a new start might not be enough to banish her troubles. Period drama, starring Keeley Hawes, based on Gerald Durrell's trilogy of Corfu memoirs, which includes My Family and Other Animals(n)

programme.desc: Jimmy Bullard and Jamie O'Hara join team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey at Anglers Paradise in Devon, trying to catch ornamental koi carp, blue orfe and golden tench(n)

programme.title: Football Rivalries
programme.desc: Featuring Manchester United and Liverpool(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A bloody attack on a young swimmer points to something dangerous in a remote stretch of the Parana River in Argentina. Jeremy Wade discovers locals are blaming a creature they call the River Tiger - a muscular predator with jaws like a bolt cutter - but to back up their stories, the freshwater detective needs to catch a specimen big enough to inflict the chilling wounds he has heard about(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The super-sleuth visits a village to investigate a number of mysterious deaths, and tries to prove they have a logical rather than a supernatural explanation. Feature-length drama, starring Jeremy Brett, with Roy Marsden and Keith Barron(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 3.00 Warwickshire Classic Handicap Steeple Chase, and races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.35. Plus, races from 2.05, 2.40 and 3.15 at Kempton Park. Here, he heads to the River Danube in search of two giant beasts - a fish so valuable criminals are hunting it out of existence and a man-sized predator that has become the stuff of dreams. programme.title: Nevada Smith (1966)
programme.desc: The son of a Native American mother and a white father dedicates his life to gaining revenge on the three outlaws who murdered them. Taken in by a gun merchant who educates him in firearms and instructs him how to find the men, he sets out in pursuit. Western adventure, starring Steve McQueen, Karl Malden, Brian Keith, Arthur Kennedy and Suzanne Pleshette(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess takes up a new position, but feels constrained to consult Holmes about some of the bizarre conditions stipulated by her employer. Period crime drama, guest starring Natasha Richardson, Joss Ackland and Patience Collier, with Jeremy Brett and David Burke(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur becomes a rebel without a clue by launching a motorbike despatch agency - but Ray is concerned the entire venture is heading for a crash. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle learn an assassin has been hired to murder one of the delegates at an anti-terrorist conference and set about discovering the identity of the killer's target. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A series of damning reports claims police officers are not playing by the book, so Regan poses as an ex-con and heads to a club frequented by two officers suspected of running a protection racket and taking bribes from villains to release them. John Thaw stars(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A racehorse unaccountably goes wild and kills its jockey in full view of Quincy - but an autopsy convinces him the death was murder rather than a freak accident(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo is convinced that the suspects in the murder of a book-keeper are innocent, but his attempts to prove it lead him into conflict with the FBI, who have their own agenda(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An interpreter claims to have been abducted by two villains who forced him to interrogate a badly bruised and obviously distressed Greek captive unable to speak English. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is offered the chance to buy a cheap Daimler, but while trying to locate its dealer's elusive premises is arrested for kerb-crawling. With a gross of red light bulbs in his boot, the evidence seems compelling, and even Ray begins to doubt his uncle's innocence. Nevertheless, the loyal nephew sets out to track down the so-called Goodtime garage to provide corroboration. Lighthearted drama, with George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect Cowley's former girlfriend, now a controversial evangelist, from an assassin, but are unaware that the would-be hitman is right under their nose. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A youth is arrested after an elderly money-lender is brutally assaulted and robbed. His mother asks Regan, an old friend, to intervene. Though it is later proved he got a legitimate loan and is innocent of theft, the youngster finds himself being blackmailed after falling foul of some very dangerous individuals. Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Angellica Bell, Oritse Williams, Leslie Ash and Ian Lavender pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: Members of the Kerry Mountain Rescue team head to Mangerton mountain after receiving a call about an elderly walker in Killarney National Park who has complained of chest pains. On the Humber estuary, a pleasure boat suffers engine failure and Humber rescue team members speed to its aid while it drifts perilously close to some rocks(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Croagh Patrick in Co Mayo, the Mayo Mountain Rescue Team gears up for the annual religious pilgrimage, where up to 30,000 people set out to reach the summit of the mountain. More than 120 volunteers from other rescue teams, the Order of Malta, Civil Defence and Irish Air Corps assist the many injured pilgrims as they fall, have heart attacks and break ankles. On the Kinder Plateau, the Buxton and Kinder Mountain Rescue teams are called to an injured walker, while in the Lake District, three mountain rescue teams attend an injured ice climber with spinal and head injuries(n)

programme.title: Fighter Pilot: The Real Top Gun
programme.desc: Documentary following three trainees at Britain's elite fighter pilot school, including the establishment's only female student. In the first episode, the pilots are put through their paces learning to fly the new F35 Lightning stealth jet. These planes can avoid radar, hover and land vertically, but with only a few months to bring them to the UK and prepare them for active service, the stakes a re high for the aspiring pilots(n)

programme.title: Carlito's Way (1993)
programme.desc: A former gangster is released from jail and vows to go straight, hoping to earn enough money to escape his New York roots - but his misguided loyalty to a crooked lawyer threatens to drag him back into a life of crime. Brian De Palma's thriller, starring Al Pacino, Sean Penn, Penelope Ann Miller, John Leguizamo and Viggo Mortensen(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An ailing diplomat asks Holmes to retrieve a document about a highly sensitive treaty between Britain and Italy, which an opportunistic thief has stolen from his desk at the Foreign Office. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett, David Burke and David Gwillim(n)

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Jimmy Bullard and Jamie O'Hara join team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey at Anglers Paradise in Devon, trying to catch ornamental koi carp, blue orfe and golden tench(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel to the Rio Ebro in Spain, where they target specimen roach and carp, and face the challenge of catching a giant wels catfish in a test of their skill and strength. They are joined on the final leg of their journey by globetrotting angler Gary Newman(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Rishi Persad and the team look ahead to the day's racing at Warwick(n)

programme.title: Football Rivalries
programme.desc: Featuring Manchester United and Liverpool(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A bloody attack on a young swimmer points to something dangerous in a remote stretch of the Parana River in Argentina. Jeremy Wade discovers locals are blaming a creature they call the River Tiger - a muscular predator with jaws like a bolt cutter - but to back up their stories, the freshwater detective needs to catch a specimen big enough to inflict the chilling wounds he has heard about(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The super-sleuth visits a village to investigate a number of mysterious deaths, and tries to prove they have a logical rather than a supernatural explanation. Feature-length drama, starring Jeremy Brett, with Roy Marsden and Keith Barron(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 3.00 Warwickshire Classic Handicap Steeple Chase, and races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.35. Plus, races from 2.05, 2.40 and 3.15 at Kempton Park. With analysis from Mick Fitzgerald and Luke Harvey, reports by Rishi Persad, Alice Plunkett and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: The Untouchables (1987)
programme.desc: Incorruptible federal agent Eliot Ness is assigned the task of fighting organised crime in Prohibition-era Chicago and bringing the notorious crime boss Al Capone to justice. Brian De Palma's crime thriller, with Kevin Costner, Robert De Niro, Andy Garcia and Sean Connery, who won an Oscar for his performance(n)

programme.title: Passenger 57 (1992)
programme.desc: After his wife is shot dead during a robbery, a cop hands in his badge and takes up a job offer from an old friend to be a security adviser for a major transatlantic airline. On the flight taking him to his new employers in Los Angeles is an international terrorist being escorted to trial by the FBI - while the prisoner's accomplices are disguised as passengers and crew and plan to hijack the plane. Action thriller, with Wesley Snipes, Bruce Payne, Tom Sizemore, Alex Datcher, Bruce Greenwood and Elizabeth Hurley(n)

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: There appears to be evidence that some of the world's most iconic rivers are in declining health, prompting biologist, extreme angler and naturalist Jeremy Wade to investigate the disappearance of freshwater giants from the planet's largest waterways. Here, he heads to the River Danube in search of two giant beasts - a fish so valuable criminals are hunting it out of existence and a man-sized predator that has become the stuff of dreams. What does the fate of these creatures say about the health of Europe's second longest river?(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes disguises himself as a plumber to gain access to the home of a blackmailing art dealer who targets members of high society. Robert Hardy guest stars in a feature-length episode of the period drama, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke(n)

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Promoted to Acting Lieutenant, Hornblower is soon forced to question his readiness for command. Meanwhile, the Indefatigable encounters another British ship that has been attacked by a Spanish frigate, leaving only three survivors _ one of whom is a notorious captain known to Pellew. Second of four 18th-century seafaring adventures based on the stories by CS Forester, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay, Denis Lawson, Ian McNeice and Andrew Tiernan(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George gets to celebrate one of his favourite periods of British architecture when he helps out at an arts and crafts Edwardian terrace house in Addiscombe, south London. Cookie and Sophia bought their home six months ago, but, despite it being full to the brim with amazing original features, from fireplaces to balustrades and stained-glass windows, it needed some serious TLC. This renovation is a labour of love as the couple set about painstakingly restoring the features by themselves. George visits the wallpaper library of his all-time design hero William Morris, and tries his hand at block printing(n)

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: The 22 remaining recruits, including a mole who is an undercover DS, are sent deeper into the epic and unforgiving terrain of the Isle of Raasay in the Scottish Highlands, to see if they have the mental and physical robustness required to pass this unique version of SAS selection. This stage of the course sees the exodus of four of the recruits as the ex-special forces' instructors push them to control their aggression. Estate agent James Quantrell hosts the first evening, with a Best of British theme, and the group dynamics soon become a talking point. Project manager Sandhya Gadsby is not impressed by James's tales of public-school antics or pensioner Cherry Hughes' old-fashioned values, while Cherry clashes with tattoo artist Lee Jackson. As the nights unfold, the divisions become clear, but is it a class divide or something more surprising?(n)

programme.title: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: When elderly teacher Peter Farquhar was found dead at home in the village of Maids Moreton in 2015, it was sad and unexpected, but no one thought it suspicious. But 18 months later, when his neighbour Ann Moore-Martin also fell ill and died, the police decided to look again. They found that Ben Field, a would-be vicar and academic, had recently become part of both of their lives and benefited from their wills. This programme shows that by delving back in time through friends' and loved ones' memories of Peter and Ben's relationship, as well as Peter's diary, the police discovered a story of love and betrayal(n)

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands examine the history of HMS Invincible, which was captured from the French in 1747 and helped transform the Royal Navy with the lessons it taught about organisation and ship design. For more than 260 years, HMS Invincible lay at the bottom of the Solent, but archaeologists are now diving beneath the waves in a £2m excavation to discover more about this game-changing ship(n)

programme.title: Tony Robinson's History of Britain
programme.desc: The actor explores the lives of people from the Victorian era, including a 13-year-old girl who worked 14-hour shifts in a Bryant & May factory in London's East End, a woman who worked down a mine at Knowles Pitt near Bolton and a navvy who blasted tons of rock from the Highlands to help construct an ambitious water channel. Tony also hears the story of one of the first employees of Marks & Spencer.(n)

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Michael Buerk learns how the Victorians built the biggest, fastest, most technologically advanced ships in the world, which helped the nation to dominate the high seas. In Portsmouth, he boards Queen Victoria's flagship HMS Warrior to reveal surprising construction secrets and the ways the ship kept the peace in international waters, before following in Brunel's footsteps aboard his luxury steamer SS Great Britain(n)

programme.title: The Story of
programme.desc: Profile of the self-effacing funnyman, who entered the public consciousness after landing a main part in ITV drama Fat Friends. He later enjoyed roles in major films by Shane Meadows and Mike Leigh, but it was being cast in Alan Bennett's seminal play The History Boys that set the stage for his meteoric rise. James then called on fellow Fat Friends alumnus Ruth Jones to join him in writing a sitcom that was destined to be a classic - Gavin & Stacey. An enthusiastically received appearance on Broadway prompted US TV execs to cherry-pick him to front The Late Late Show(n)

programme.title: World's Most Luxurious Trains
programme.desc: A look at top-end trains, such as South Africa's famous Blue Train. A five-star hotel on wheels, guests pay upwards of £2,600 per ticket for the 40-hour journey from Pretoria to Cape Town, enjoying 24-hour butler service, bespoke linen and a soak in their own private bath. Another South African luxury train, Rovos Rail, offers a game drive. In Russia, the Golden Eagle offer a Trans-Siberian train journey, which crosses nine time zones on its two-week journey. The Presidential Train offers a luxury daytrip through the Portuguese countryside, where guests are served a meal created by a Michelin-starred chef(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath(n)

Animated adventure, with the voices of Robin Williams, Christian Slater and Tim Curry(n)

David Lynch's drama, starring Richard Farnsworth, Sissy Spacek, Jane Galloway, Everett McGill and Harry Dean Stanton(n)

Drama, starring Keke Palmer, Laurence Fishburne and Angela Bassett(n)

Adventure, starring Nicholas Rowe, Alan Cox and Sophie Ward(n)

Comedy, starring Eddie Murphy, Ossie Davis and Oliver Platt(n)

programme.title: Split Interview Special

Rachel thinks she has vital evidence in cracking the case, but gaps in her memory cloud her judgment, and before long her own life is endangered. Thriller based on Paula Hawkins' novel, starring Emily Blunt, Rebecca Ferguson, Haley Bennett, Justin Theroux and Luke Evans(n)

programme.title: Leaving Neverland: Michael Jackson and Me
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part documentary exploring the experiences of James Safechuck and Wade Robson, who were both befriended by Michael Jackson as young boys. They and their families were invited into his singular and wondrous world, entranced by the singer's fairy-tale existence as his career reached its peak. Through interviews with Safechuck and Robson, now in their 30s and 40s, as well as their mothers, wives and siblings, director Dan Reed's film presents a portrait of sustained abuse, and explores the complicated feelings that led both men to confront their experiences(n)
